narration: Money is the number one cause of divorce in America — and it's not just about not having enough. If you and your partner fight about spending, you're losing way more than the argument.
visual: Dark background. Two person silhouettes face each other with a red dollar sign between them. Text: 'MONEY IS THE #1 CAUSE OF DIVORCE'. Both figures slide in from opposite sides.

narration: Seventy percent of couples argue about money regularly. And here's what nobody tells you — those fights don't just cost your relationship. They cost you thousands of dollars every single year.
visual: Light background. Two speech bubbles from opposite sides — one says SPEND, one says SAVE. A large '70%' counter animates upward. Subtitle: 'OF COUPLES FIGHT ABOUT MONEY'.

narration: When you're financially misaligned, you make competing purchases, miss investment windows, and avoid hard money conversations. That scattered approach leaks thousands every year — and it compounds.
visual: Dark background. Left side: person with shopping bag (SPEND). Right side: person with piggy bank (SAVE). Dollar bills fly off in opposite directions. Text: 'THOUSANDS LOST EVERY YEAR'.

narration: Kansas State University found couples who fight about money are 37% more likely to divorce. And divorce doesn't just hurt emotionally — the average split costs between fifteen and thirty thousand dollars in legal fees alone.
visual: Light background. Gavel SVG with a swing animation. '37% MORE LIKELY TO DIVORCE' appears. Below, a cost counter climbs from $0 to $30,000 inside a dark box labeled 'AVERAGE DIVORCE COST'.

narration: Even if you stay together, misaligned couples retire with seven hundred sixty thousand dollars less than financially compatible ones. That gap comes from wasted spending, missed compound growth, and years of financial conflict.
visual: Dark background. Two bar charts side by side grow upward — green bar (ALIGNED, $1.2M) towers over red bar (MISALIGNED, $440K). '$760,000 GAP' badge springs in at the end.

narration: The fix isn't finding someone who spends exactly like you. It's scheduling a monthly money date — just thirty minutes to align on goals. That one habit closes the gap. Start tonight.
visual: Light background. Calendar SVG with a heart marked on a date. Two person silhouettes sit together. CTA button: 'SCHEDULE YOUR MONEY DATE'. Pulsing 'START TONIGHT →' arrow.
